review: UX-REVIEW-7 (verification of the UX-REVIEW-6 fix round)
skip_endorsed: Audition waveform in patch picker: skip is correct (C plumbing + cross-core buffer copies for a decoration); fake-envelope stand-in also not worth it.

H1.note: reset_cause logging works (caught a live WDT this pass); chunked tab builds held; but reboots still occur -- see NEW-1

H2.note: Name + typed text centered; placeholders still dim (NEW-4)

M2.note: bonus channel map + live conflict warning; disabled hues residual (NEW-3)

M3.note: style IS green on the live widget; deferred-refill region renders stale until invalidate; see NEW-2

M4.note: list resizes+scrolls above keyboard, but still 1 visible row; see NEW-5

L8.note: 32KB internal alloc fails on every keyboard open; implicated in NEW-1

N5.note: new MPE conflict copy adds another '--'

MOD.note: MIDI monitor live-verified; icons, status line, mono values, card edges; waveform skip endorsed

findings.title: Keyboard-open on the patch picker can still WDT-reboot the device (intermittent, ~1 in 2 this bench)
findings.summary: Focus search -> auto-keyboard -> device rebooted; deck.log recorded '=== deck boot === reset_cause=WDT' (new logging working). 'alloc failed size 32768 internal 8bit' fires on EVERY keyboard open. Hypothesis: the keyboard-flash fix (cb5f7e3f) allocates an internal-SRAM PARTIAL draw buffer on keyboard open; that 32KB alloc fails, rendering falls into a slow path exactly while keyboard build + M4 list resize churn peaks, opening the WDT window.
evidence: serial alloc failure on every open

location.file: tulip firmware PARTIAL keyboard buffer (cb5f7e3f) + deck/instrument.py list resize

findings.suggested_fix: Reserve the 32KB internal buffer once at boot (or degrade gracefully when it fails); resize the existing listbody on keyboard open instead of rebuilding rows; window the patch list (~20 rows, fill on scroll).

findings.title: Deferred-refill panels render stale pixels (root cause of M3); one-line fix
findings.summary: After back()'s deferred refill, the rack switch renders theme-blue although the live widget's resolved indicator color probes green (63,191,127) and CHECKED. Forcing lv.screen_active().invalidate() repaints it green. Panels rebuilt inside tulip.defer ticks are not invalidated after styling.
evidence: live style probe in review transcript
findings.suggested_fix: Call h.invalidate() after self._fill(h, b) in _schedule_refill._do. Keep the two-selector switch styling as belt-and-braces.

findings.title: Disabled-state recolor produces alarm hues (magenta/crimson/olive) on MPE sliders, Files footer, channel map
findings.summary: LVGL DISABLED gray-mix + RGB332 quantization turns teal/orange/accent into danger-looking colors on inactive controls; a disabled pitch-bend slider reads crimson.
findings.suggested_fix: Explicit disabled styles: track/bg SURFACE2, text MUTED, knob GRAY -- never rely on the theme's color mixing.

findings.title: Placeholder contrast still below arm's-length legibility (patch search, both Wi-Fi fields)
findings.suggested_fix: Placeholder one step lighter than MUTED (fields sit on SURFACE2).

findings.title: Keyboard mode should collapse the picker header; only 1 result row visible while typing
findings.suggested_fix: Hide title/current/Favorites block while keyboard is up; ~3 rows become visible.

findings.title: Files: Delete renders muted purple when enabled (should be the destructive red); footer filename is placeholder-dim
findings.suggested_fix: Delete = dk.RED when enabled; filename label = TEXT not MUTED.

findings.title: MIDI monitor timestamps are raw boot ticks; relative seconds or delta-ms would serve debugging better
findings.suggested_fix: Show t-relative-to-first-message (or delta since previous event).

findings.title: Fix round left test residue on the device (extra 'Tulip 2' instrument configured)
findings.summary: Bench verification ended without restoring device state. Restored this pass to the true pre-review config.
findings.suggested_fix: End every on-device verification with the restore step the review passes use (backup config first, restore + reboot after).
